channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Hawaii man freed after 30 years in prison visits mother's grave and ponders ubiquitous cellphones
item.description: HONOLULU (AP) — One of the first places Gordon Cordeiro visited when a judge ordered him released after spending 30 years in prison for a killing he always maintained he had nothing to do with was his mother’s Hawaii gravesite.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER SINCO KELLEHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Border Patrol agent whose death is tied to cultlike Zizians is buried with military honors
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A U.S. Border Patrol agent whose killing in Vermont during a traffic stop near the Canadian border has been tied to a cultlike group was buried with full military honors Saturday at a national cemetery in Minnesota. David Maland, 44, died Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Briscoe tries to move past devastating penalties and Blaney to start on pole in NASCAR Atlanta race
item.description: A massive fine left Chase Briscoe facing a huge points deficit heading into Sunday's NASCAR Cup Series race at Atlanta Motor Speedway.
item.dc:creator: Charles Odum, Fox 5

item.title: Can you spot a credit card skimmer? What to look for
item.description: Before putting your card into a reader, the FBI says there are some easy and effective ways to protect your information.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Woman with 'burning' sensation in legs diagnosed with brain worm infection
item.description: A New England woman thought she was just dealing with jet lag after an extended vacation, but medical experts later diagnosed her symptoms as an infection from brain worms.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: Trump spars with Maine's governor at the White House over transgender athletes
item.description: President Donald Trump sparred with Maine's Democratic governor during a meeting of governors at the White House on Friday, with Gov. Janet Mills telling the Republican president, “We'll see you in court,” over his push to deny federal funding to the state over transgender athletes.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chaotic scene unfolded as 3 were shot and killed outside Kentucky driver's license office
item.description: LOUISVILLE, Ky. (AP) — Witnesses described a chaotic scene in which three people were shot and killed outside of a Kentucky driver's license office, including one man shot multiple times by masked assailants.
item.dc:creator: DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Venezuelan family in US under curtailed humanitarian protections clings to faith amid uncertainty
item.description: HOPKINS, Minn. (AP) — Every Sunday, Johann Teran goes to worship at a Lutheran service in suburban Minneapolis, trying to find some hope that the future he was building isn’t all slipping away.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Warren Buffett offers Donald Trump some advice while celebrating Berkshire Hathaway's success
item.description: Warren Buffett is celebrating the successes of Berkshire Hathaway’s companies last year and in the 60 years since he took over a struggling New England textile company and began converting it into a massive conglomerate.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Tesla recalls over 376,000 vehicles due to power steering defect
item.description: The recall involves some 2023 Tesla Model 3s and some 2023 Tesla Model Ys, according to the recall report from the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, WGN 9

item.title: Prosecutors: Presidential pardon for Jan. 6 does not apply to Maryville man's FBI ‘kill list’ plot conviction
item.description: Prosecutors have responded to a motion filed by a Maryville man to dismiss his conviction in to a 2022 plot to murder federal investigators at the FBI Knoxville Field Office after he was pardoned for his role in the January 6, 2021 insurrection at the U.S. Capitol.
item.dc:creator: Hope McAlee, NewsNation

item.title: LA mayor dismisses fire chief over response to most destructive wildfire in city history last month
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Six weeks after the start of the most destructive wildfire in city history, Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass ousted the fire chief Friday amid a public rift over preparations for a potential blaze and finger-pointing between the chief and City Hall over responsibility for the devastat
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. BLOOD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Luigi Mangione makes first court appearance since arraignment in UnitedHealthcare CEO's death
item.description: Luigi Mangione, 26, has pleaded not guilty to multiple counts of murder, including murder as an act of terrorism, in the Dec. 4 killing of Brian Thompson outside a midtown Manhattan hotel.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, KTLA 5

item.title: Supreme Court won’t allow Trump to immediately fire head of whistleblower office
item.description: The justices said in an unsigned order that Hampton Dellinger, head of the Office of Special Counsel, could remain in his job at least until Feb. 26. That’s when a lower-court order temporarily protecting him expires.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Pentagon says it will cut 5,400 probationary workers starting next week
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Defense Department said Friday that it’s cutting 5,400 probationary workers starting next week and will put a hiring freeze in place.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: A New Jersey man is convicted of attempted murder in the stabbing of Salman Rushdie
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — A New Jersey man was convicted Friday of attempted murder for stabbing author Salman Rushdie multiple times on a New York lecture stage in 2022.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Florida woman who told insurance compant 'delay, deny, depose' has charge dropped
item.description: The charge against Lakeland woman accused of threatening her health insurance company was dropped on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Video shows barefoot foster child with no coat left in snow
item.description: A man in Haskell, Oklahoma, called 911 after hearing a child outside in the cold begging to go back inside a residence. Rhet Wike also captured video of the little girl standing outside in record-breaking freezing temperatures and wearing only pajamas.
item.dc:creator: Heather Holeman/KFOR, KTLA 5

item.title: One-armed basketball player makes history with basket
item.description: When her high school basketball coach cut her from the team senior year, Baileigh Sinaman-Daniel refused to let that rejection, or the fact she was born with one arm, end her dream.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Witness to Idaho murders says intruder with bushy eyebrows carried vacuum out of crime scene: court docs
item.description: A surviving University of Idaho housemate told detectives she saw a masked man with bushy eyebrows carrying what looked like a vacuum away from the scene.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Connor McDavid scores in OT to give Canada 3-2 win over United States in 4 Nations Face-Off final
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — When they played “O Canada” for the second time, there were no American fans left in the arena to boo.
item.dc:creator: JIMMY GOLEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Grand jury recommends Alabama police department be abolished
item.description: The small Alabama city of Hanceville, located about 45 miles north of Birmingham, placed its entire police force on administrative leave a day after the grand jury recommendation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Banfield, KRON4

item.title: Elon Musk calls for 'deorbiting' International Space Station to prioritize Mars missions
item.description: Elon Musk advocated for "deorbiting" the International Space Station "as soon as possible," suggesting "2 years from now." He said the space station "has served its purpose.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Singer of Canadian anthem at 4 Nations Face-Off changes lyric to protest Trump's 51st state remarks
item.description: The anthem singer who performed the Canadian anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off championship game Thursday night changed a lyric in “O Canada” as a response to U.S. President Donald Trump's repeated remarks about making the country the 51st state.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO and KEN POWTAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Luigi Mangione winks at supporters with green sweater under bulletproof vest
item.description: Legal experts say voir dire, or jury selection, could be the key to deciding the fate of suspected UnitedHealthcare CEO assassin Luigi Mangione.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Nearly 200 Venezuelan migrants are flown home from Guantanamo Bay, with a layover in Honduras
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Nearly 200 Venezuelan immigrants to the U.S. were returned to their home country after being detained at Guantanamo Bay, in a flurry of flights that forged an unprecedented pathway for U.S. deportations. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MORGAN LEE and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The man accused of stabbing Salman Rushdie declines to take the stand as the defense rests
item.description: MAYVILLE, N.Y. (AP) — The New Jersey man on trial in the 2022 stabbing of author Salman Rushdie declined to testify in his defense Thursday as his lawyers rested their case without calling any witnesses.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Some New York prison guards charged in beating death of handcuffed inmate appear in court
item.description: UTICA, N.Y. (AP) — Six New York prison guards have been indicted for second-degree murder in the beating death of a handcuffed inmate, a brutal incident captured on body-worn cameras that triggered widespread outrage and calls for justice.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Forever 21 may close remaining stores as second bankruptcy looms
item.description: Fashion retailer Forever 21 has begun liquidation sales at several of its locations throughout the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, WFLA, NBC
